perturbation.original_text: During the course of such Discussions and any subsequent negotiations (the " Negotiations "), either party may have need of information from the other party that is regarded as confidential or may constitute a trade secret or other proprietary corporate information. Accordingly, the parties agree as follows: 1. Confidential Information. (a) " Confidential Information " as used in this Agreement is defined as written or oral information or materials, in whatever form, that (i) is disclosed by one party (the " Disclosing Party ") to the other party (the " Receiving Party ") in connection with the Discussions and/or the Negotiations; and (ii) relates to the business and/or affairs of the Disclosing Party, and includes, but is not limited to, the business and marketing plans and strategies, customers, suppliers, distributors, agents, research and product development plans, products, product cycles, technology, operations, assets, intellectual property, know-how, software, source code, copyright works, trade-marks, concepts, ideas, data, designs, "look and feel" design elements, specifications, inventions, algorithms, formulae, processes, techniques, systems and improvements, partnerships, joint ventures, financial statements, financial projections, models, studies, and works in progress of the Disclosing Party. (b) " Confidential Information " as used in this Agreement shall not include: (i) information that is publicly known at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or thereafter becomes publicly known (other than as a result of a breach of the obligations of the Receiving Party contained in this Agreement to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Information); (ii) information that is actually known to the Receiving Party prior to the time of receipt of such Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party; (iii) information that is furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party who, to the best of the Receiving Party's knowledge after due inquiry, is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or by an obligation of confidence to, the Disclosing Party; (iv) information that is independently acquired or developed by the Receiving Party without use of, or reference to, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and without otherwise contravening the terms and provisions of this Agreement; and (v) information in regards to which the Disclosing Party authorizes in writing to the Receiving Party their release from the confidentiality and/or limited use obligations contained in this Agreement. 1 2. Non-Disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: During the course of such Discussions and any subsequent negotiations (the " Negotiations "), either party may have need of information from the other party that is regarded as confidential or may constitute a trade secret or other proprietary corporate information. Accordingly, the parties agree as follows: 2. Non-Disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting section 1, which defines 'Confidential Information' and outlines what it includes and excludes, the term 'Confidential Information' becomes undefined. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the subsequent sections (especially Section 2, Non-Disclosure) rely on this definition to establish the obligations of the Receiving Party. Without a clear definition, it's unclear what information the Receiving Party is obligated to protect, making Section 2 unenforceable as originally written.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure. The Receiving Party shall, during the term of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years after the termination of this Agreement, use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information and to limit its disclosure to only: (a) those of its directors, officers, employees, agents or professional advisors as have a need to know that Confidential Information in order that the objectives of the Discussions and Negotiations can be achieved; and (b) to any of its affiliates or subsidiaries so long as the affiliate or subsidiary understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is being provided subject to all terms and conditions of this Agreement. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term " reasonable care " shall mean the same degree of care exercised by the Receiving Party with respect to its own information of the same nature as Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall be responsible for the compliance by any of the parties listed in subsections 2(a) and (b) above with the provisions of this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall issue written instructions to those parties listed in subsections 2(a) and (b) above requiring them to comply with the Receiving Party's obligations referred to in this Section 2 and to use the Confidential Information only to assist in achieving the objectives of the Discussions and Negotiations. Any failure on the part of any of those parties listed in subsections 2(a) and (b) above to comply with the written instructions will be deemed to be a breach of this Section 2 by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the definition of 'Confidential Information' (Section 1), which is essential for understanding and enforcing Section 2 (Non-Disclosure). Section 2 relies on the definition in Section 1 to specify what the Receiving Party is obligated to protect. Without this definition, the term becomes ambiguous, creating a direct conflict and making Section 2 unenforceable. The 'Perturbation Explanation' confirms this interpretation. Therefore, based on the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, and the explanation provided, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, at its own expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including all written information, whether originals or copies of documents, records, summaries, analyses, and notes, and all materials in its possession that contain Confidential Information, regardless of who authored or generated such written information or materials, to the Disclosing Party immediately upon their request. If any of the Confidential Information is destroyed prior to the request, an adequate response to a return request of that Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party will be a written certificate, executed by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party, certifying that the relevant Confidential Information was destroyed. Additionally, the Receiving Party shall at the same time remove all digital representations, in any form, that contain any Confidential Information, from all electronic storage media in its possession or under its control.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, at its own expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party all information, including all written information, whether originals or copies of documents, records, summaries, analyses, and notes, and all materials in its possession, regardless of who authored or generated such written information or materials, to the Disclosing Party immediately upon their request. If any of the information is destroyed prior to the request, an adequate response to a return request of that information by the Disclosing Party will be a written certificate, executed by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party, certifying that the relevant information was destroyed. Additionally, the Receiving Party shall at the same time remove all digital representations, in any form, that contain any information, from all electronic storage media in its possession or under its control.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information' in Section 6, the contract now requires the Receiving Party to return 'all information', not just confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction with the core purpose of the agreement, which is to protect *confidential* information, as stated in the initial paragraphs describing the need for the agreement. Now it seems the Receiving Party has to return all the information they have, creating a contradictory obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties are entering into discussions related to exploring the possibility of Stuart Energy and Hydrogenics cooperating in joint business initiatives for their mutual benefit, including, potentially, a business combination (the " Discussions "). During the course of such Discussions and any subsequent negotiations (the " Negotiations "), either party may have need of information from the other party that is regarded as confidential or may constitute a trade secret or other proprietary corporate information. Accordingly, the parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'Confidential Information', aligning with the agreement's purpose of protecting confidential information as described in the contradicted text. The changed text broadens the scope to 'all information', creating a contradiction. The contradicted text focuses on protecting confidential information exchanged during discussions, while the changed text now requires the return of all information, regardless of its confidentiality. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, as the Receiving Party would now be obligated to return information beyond the scope of what the agreement was intended to protect, thus leading to a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "with a view toward the consummation of any agreement" from section (b) in Section 7, the text now contradicts the initial purpose of the agreement. The agreement aimed at 'exploring the possibility' of cooperation. Removing the specific condition related to consummation implies that the parties might have exchanged other kinds of information unrelated to the actual agreement, which clashes with the overall goal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties are entering into discussions related to exploring the possibility of Stuart Energy and Hydrogenics cooperating in joint business initiatives for their mutual benefit, including, potentially, a business combination (the " Discussions "). During the course of such Discussions and any subsequent negotiations (the " Negotiations "), either party may have need of information from the other party that is regarded as confidential or may constitute a trade secret or other proprietary corporate information. Accordingly, the parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'with a view toward the consummation of any agreement' from section (b). According to the explanation provided, this implies the parties exchanged other kinds of information unrelated to the actual agreement, contradicting the stated purpose in the 'Contradicted Text', which is that discussions are related to 'exploring the possibility' of cooperation in joint business initiatives.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement does not in any way constitute a binding commitment on the part of either party to complete the Discussions or Negotiations or enter into any joint business initiative(s) or any other resulting transaction(s) with the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement does not in any way constitute a binding commitment on the part of either party to complete the Discussions or Negotiations with the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "or enter into any joint business initiative(s) or any other resulting transaction(s) with the other party" from Section 9, it now contradicts the preamble's statement that the discussions are related to 'exploring the possibility of Stuart Energy and Hydrogenics cooperating in joint business initiatives'. The modified text suggests only discussions and negotiations aren't binding, but now leaves open the interpretation that joint ventures could be binding from the onset.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties are entering into discussions related to exploring the possibility of Stuart Energy and Hydrogenics cooperating in joint business initiatives for their mutual benefit, including, potentially, a business combination (the " Discussions "). During the course of such Discussions and any subsequent negotiations (the " Negotiations "), either party may have need of information from the other party that is regarded as confidential or may constitute a trade secret or other proprietary corporate information. Accordingly, the parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that neither completing discussions/negotiations nor entering into joint business initiatives constitutes a binding commitment. The changed text removes the phrase about joint business initiatives, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the preamble which states the parties are entering into discussions related to exploring the possibility of Stuart Energy and Hydrogenics cooperating in joint business initiatives. The altered text now implies that while the discussions and negotiations aren't binding, any joint business initiative could potentially be binding from the outset, directly contradicting the explicitly non-binding nature of all discussions and initiatives mentioned in the original text and preamble. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, and each such executed counterpart will be deemed to be an original instrument, but all such counterparts together will constitute one and the same instrument. This Agreement may be executed by facsimile transmission.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, and each such executed counterpart will be deemed to be an original instrument, but all such counterparts together will constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement "This Agreement may be executed by facsimile transmission" from section 17, it now creates a contradiction with common business practices and potentially contradicts with preceding clauses that might assume quick exchange of information (e.g., in Section 14 regarding notices via fax). The absence of explicitly allowing fax execution could invalidate agreements made that way. The in-text contradiction occurs because other sections imply prompt communication/confirmation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notices. Any notice, request or consent which may or is required to be given pursuant to this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered or faxed as follows: if to Stuart Energy, to Stuart Energy Systems Corporation, 5101 Orbitor Drive, Mississauga, Ontario, L4W 4V1, Attention: R. Randall MacEwen, Vice President, Corporate Development, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.282.7701, or if to Hydrogenics, to Hydrogenics Corporation, 5985 McLaughlin Road, Mississauga, Ontario L5R 1B8, Attention: Jonathan Lundy, Vice President, Strategic Development and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.361.3626 or to such other address as the relevant party may from time to time advise by notice in writing given in accordance with this Section 14. The time and date of receipt of any such notice, request or consent will be deemed to be the time and date of the actual receipt thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed execution by facsimile, which is directly relevant to Section 14 concerning notices. The changed text removes this explicit allowance. Section 14 specifies facsimile as a method for delivering notices. By removing the explicit allowance for facsimile execution in the changed text, it creates a direct contradiction with Section 14, as it's unclear whether notices sent via fax would be considered valid or enforceable. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction,' where removing essential language creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.
